purpose: Negative conformance fixtures — cases that MUST fail validation. A conformant verifier that accepts any of these vectors has a bug. Two failure modes: (1) missing required preimage field, (2) tampered action_ref hash.
validation_rule: A conformant implementation MUST reject any action_ref presented as valid if: (a) the preimage is missing any of the four required fields [action_type, agent_id, scope, timestamp], or (b) the action_ref does not equal SHA-256(JCS(preimage)).

vectors.description: Preimage is missing the required 'scope' field. The broken_action_ref is the SHA-256 of the incomplete preimage (3 fields only). A verifier MUST reject this: the broken_action_ref cannot be verified against a complete preimage, and the complete preimage produces a different, non-matching hash. Any implementation that accepts broken_action_ref as valid for the complete preimage has a conformance bug.
vectors.verifier_must: REJECT any claim that broken_action_ref is a valid action_ref for the complete_preimage. The two hashes differ because scope was omitted from the JCS input.

vectors.description: action_ref has been tampered: last nibble flipped from '4' to '0'. The preimage is intact and well-formed. A verifier MUST re-derive SHA-256(JCS(preimage)) and compare against the presented action_ref — they will not match. Any implementation that accepts the tampered_action_ref without re-deriving the hash has a conformance bug.
vectors.tamper_description: Last nibble flipped: '4' → '0'. Single-character mutation — a minimal but detectable forgery attempt.
vectors.verifier_must: RE-DERIVE SHA-256(JCS(preimage)) and assert it equals the presented action_ref. A verifier that skips re-derivation and trusts the presented hash is trivially forgeable.
